---
title: Teaching
description: "Teaching and supervision by Dr Justin C Yang at UCL, covering epidemiology, statistics, and open science at the postgraduate level."

output:
  html_document:
    keep_md: yes
---
Dr Yang primarily teaches epidemiology, statistics, and open science at the postgraduate level. He has taught on the [Core Principles of Mental Health Research](https://www.ucl.ac.uk/module-catalogue/modules/core-principles-of-mental-health-research-PSBS0002), [Practical Statistics for Mental Health Research](https://www.ucl.ac.uk/module-catalogue/modules/practical-statistics-for-mental-health-research-PSBS0008), and [Epidemiological Research Methods in Mental Health](https://www.ucl.ac.uk/module-catalogue/modules/epidemiological-research-methods-in-mental-health-PSBS0013) modules at UCL. More recently, he has co-developed curricula around open science and public involvement and engagement with research.

Dr Yang’s teaching is shaped by the belief that epidemiology and psychiatric research matter because they influence how we understand inequality, improve care, and support people to live meaningful and fulfilling lives. He teaches quantitative methods not simply as a set of technical skills, but as a way of thinking carefully about evidence: what a study can and cannot tell us, when stronger or causal conclusions are justified, and how findings can be communicated clearly and honestly to diverse audiences. Drawing on his own research, he uses real examples to show how analytical decisions can change the answers we reach, while keeping questions of fairness and equity firmly in view. He is also especially mindful of students who feel less confident with quantitative methods, and works to build confidence through practical, step-by-step problem-solving grounded in real-world questions.

## Supervision
Dr Yang welcomes enquiries from prospective PhD, MSc, and undergraduate students interested in psychiatric epidemiology, causal inference, mental health data science, and related areas. Please [get in touch](../contact/) to discuss potential supervision, project ideas, or opportunities for collaboration.

<div class="site-table-wrap">
```{r results = 'asis',echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)

read_csv('students.csv') |>
  arrange(desc(`Year(s)`), Student) |>
  knitr::kable(format = "html", table.attr = 'class="site-table"')
```
</div>
